# Delta-T 500-Year Checkpoints

Status: verified in the project `.venv`

Purpose
-------
This appendix records the full 500-year checkpoint table for the Moon's
Delta-T perturbation symmetry challenge across the supported DE441 ephemeris
interior.

Method
------
- Checkpoints are sampled at `year-01-01 12:00 TT` every 500 years.
- Rows are included only when the TT epoch falls inside the shared public
  planetary coverage used by Moira's main ephemeris paths.
- `UT JD` is the corresponding UT epoch from `tt_to_ut()`.
- `baseline Delta-T (s)` is the exact TT minus UT offset at that checkpoint.
- `dLon +1s` means Moon longitude shift when Delta-T is increased by 1 second.
- `dLon -1s` means Moon longitude shift when Delta-T is decreased by 1 second.
- `symmetry residual` is `abs(dLon(+1s) + dLon(-1s))`.
- RA/Dec columns are topocentric values for latitude `51.5`, longitude `-0.1`.
- `angular ratio` compares the on-sky RA/Dec displacement to the expected
  one-second angular rate from the Moon's reported longitude speed.

| Year | TT JD | UT JD | baseline Delta-T (s) | dLon +1s (deg) | dLon -1s (deg) | symmetry residual (deg) | dRA +1s (deg) | dRA -1s (deg) | dDec +1s (deg) | dDec -1s (deg) | angular ratio +1s | angular ratio -1s |
